codeReview.summary: Implementation is correct and comprehensive. Pattern promotion thresholds (≥5 confirmations with confidence >0.6) and Rule promotion thresholds (≥10 confirmations with confidence >0.8) are properly implemented with 1.5x retrieval rank boost for promoted items. 15 unit tests cover the feature including boundary conditions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the Rust skill procedure correctly. The implementation uses standard Rust patterns (async_trait, thiserror, chrono for datetime, serde for serialization) consistent with the codebase conventions documented in AGENTS.md.

No convention violations detected. Worker correctly used PromotionThresholds::default() for configurable thresholds, implemented Display trait for PromotionStatus enum, and followed the existing AntiPattern and Convention struct patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/pattern_learning.rs - All new code follows existing patterns

sharedStateObservations.observation: No additional services were started. Worker used standard build/test commands already documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands: cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-memory -- promotion, cargo clippy --workspace

summary: Feature implementation reviewed and passes scrutiny. Pattern promotion (≥5 confirmations, confidence >0.6) and rule promotion (≥10 confirmations, confidence >0.8) correctly implemented with 1.5x retrieval rank boost. Comprehensive tests cover boundary conditions (exactly 0.6/0.8 vs just above), confirmation count edges (4 vs 5, 9 vs 10), and promotion status transitions. Worker followed Rust skill procedure without deviations.
